title: oenocarpus mapora subsp. dryanderae
intro: Oenocarpus mapora subsp. dryanderae, also known as the New Zealand soapbark tree, is a plant species native to New Zealand, characterized by its smooth, white bark and distinctive rounded leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, due to the presence of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Oenocarpus mapora subsp. dryanderae include reduced inflammation, improved skin clarity, and relief from skin irritations. Traditionally, the bark of the plant is used to make soap and other herbal preparations, such as infusions and salves, which can be applied topically to the skin to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
